title: Category Specialist – Singapore Terminal and Lubricant Plant / Infrastructure (Contingent)
description:
Requisition ID: R000068387 Job Location: Singapore – Duo Tower Office

To submit application, please click below posting link or copy and paste to a new browser: https://chevron.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com/jobs/job/Singapore-DUO-Tower-Singapore/Category-Specialist---Singapore-Terminal-and-Lubricant-Plant---Infrastructure_R000068387

The Category Specialist is responsible for managing the full spectrum of the Procurement processes and activities, providing category management leadership, functional guidance, and coordination for all aspects of procurement to the International Fuel & Lubricants business in Singapore. The scope of the Procurement categories includes maintenance, engineering works, capital projects, MRO, equipment and others general services.

The role will require close collaboration and positive relationship building with management, business stakeholders, regional/local Procurement team and external vendors to deliver commercial value in support of Chevron’s business objectives.

The ideal candidate thinks strategically and collaborative but also work with significant autonomy; a strong individual who can drive category strategies and has proven track record of managing multiple initiatives and driving them to completion to meet business objectives; communicates effectively and strong ability to influence business stakeholders and suppliers at all levels and have a high level of customer focus and business judgement.

Responsibilities for this position may include but are not limited to:

  • Manage a portfolio of assigned categories, which includes:

  • Category analysis – conducts supply market analysis and market benchmarking

  • Develop country annual contracting plan to align with business plan objectives. Regular engagement with stakeholders to review and prioritize projects

  • Develop procurement strategies to execute sustainable cost savings for the business. Ensure that strategic sourcing principles are consistently used to drive best practices

  • Sourcing and negotiation - utilizes strategic sourcing process and techniques to ensure competitive advantage.  Leads negotiations or assists the lead negotiator in developing and applying appropriate negotiation strategies and tactics

  • Write contracts. Implementation of agreements and integration of key suppliers - develops transition plans and oversees implementation of new agreements in conjunction with relevant business experts

  • Supplier performance monitoring. Develop mechanism and metrics to hold suppliers accountable for performance and delivery

  • Ongoing supplier and contract management - develops supplier relationships to ensure that cost reduction and value creation are ongoing activities. Utilizes strategic cost management tools and trend forecasts to assist in maximizing value of supplier relationships and contracts

  • Set annual cost improvement targets and track results

  • Provides functional guidance to the business stakeholders and drives P2P compliance

  • Executing end-to-end procurement process in alignment and compliant with the company policies and procedures

  • Acts as a procurement advisor providing category guidance and implementation of global/regional initiatives at a local level.

  • Provide advice and consultation to business stakeholders on procurement and contract management policies.

  • Provide P2P training and work closely with stakeholders to increase P2P fluency.

  • Support Regional Category Managers in driving cost management opportunities and special projects for the business

  • Work collaboratively as a business partner to explore and implement any cost savings and/or efficiency management opportunities

  • Regularly communicates plans and progress to ensure alignment and influence the broad adoption of cost reduction strategies.

  • Leverage technologies or data analytical platforms to enhance procurement performance and process efficiency.

  • Drive synergy and enterprise sourcing initiatives.

  • Support special projects, i.e., sustainability programs

Required Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in engineering, business, supply chain or related fields.  

  • Preferably 1-5 years of Procurement experience. Procurement experience in terminal and/or blend plant environment would be a plus. Fresh graduates are welcome to apply.

  • Have an in-depth understanding of category management, with experience in category planning, contract management, supplier management and possess strong negotiation skills.

  • Experience in developing / applying total cost ownership (TCO), should cost modeling and other category management techniques in strategic sourcing.

  • Knowledge in commercial and financial analysis associated with procurement activities.

  • Strong analytical and project management skills

  • Good team player and strong drive for performance and results

  • Excellent written, verbal communication and presentation skills.

  • E-Procurement knowledge - GEP / SAP capability is a plus.

Work Location:

The selected candidate will be based in Singapore, stationed at Chevron’s Singapore office in Duo Tower. This role will require travel to the Penjuru Terminal, with time spent on-site, to provide essential support to the business.

postedCompany.description:
Singapore is the regional headquarters for chevron's downstream operations in the asia-pacific region.


Chevron has a significant presence in Singapore, dating back to 1933. The company employs around 1,100 people in the country and are involved in several key areas:


Regional Headquarters. Singapore is the regional headquarters for Chevron’s downstream operations in Asia-Pacific including manufacturing, marketing, and lubricants businesses.


Caltex Brand Management. The Caltex brand, which serves customers in Asia and the Middle East, is managed from Singapore.


Refining. Chevron has a 50 percent interest in the Singapore Refining Company (SRC).


The SRC refinery processes up to 290,000 barrels of crude oil per day, producing liquefied petroleum gas, gasoline, jet fuel, diesel, fuel oil and asphalt. Recent upgrades have enabled the refinery to produce higher-value gasoline that meets stricter emission standards.


Lubricant Additives. Chevron Oronite’s regional headquarters for Asia Pacific and home to one of the largest fuel and lubricant additives manufacturing facilities in Asia is in Singapore.


Shipping and Storage. Chevron has a key presence in Singapore’s vital shipping industry with a blending plant and a terminal with over 400,000 cubic meters of storage capacity.


Trading. Chevron’s Supply & Trading organization provides commercial support to our crude oil and natural gas production operations and to our refining and marketing network and has a regional trading hub in Singapore that partners with our headquarters in Houston, Texas and other trading hubs in London, UK and San Ramon, California.


Chevron New Energies (CNE). Is the arm of Chevron focused on developing lower carbon solutions in the Asia Pacific region.  CNE established its headquarters in Singapore 2021 and aims to help customers meet their lower carbon goals which includes carbon capture utilization and storage, hydrogen, and renewable fuels.


In Singapore, Chevron hires full-time employees with relevant education and experience in the following areas:


• Asset Commercialization

• Business Development

• Category Management

• Procurement

• Finance

• Information Technology/Data Analytics

• Shipping

• Supply & Trading


What We Offer:


• Comprehensive and competitive remuneration that are made up of short and long-term components.

• Hybrid, Flexible work-life balance options

• Ongoing learning and career development
postedCompany.name: CHEVRON SINGAPORE PTE. LTD.
